title: The Tool Use Pattern
concept: Standardizing how agents declare, call, and handle errors from external tools.

exercises.prompt: What is a way agents improve their outputs based on past performance?
exercises.explain: Agents improve their outputs by reflecting on their actions, a process that helps them learn and refine their performance.

cards.title: What is the Tool Use Pattern?
cards.body: The Tool Use Pattern is how agents communicate with external tools. It involves properly declaring, calling, and managing errors when using these tools.

cards.title: Definition of Tool Use Pattern
cards.body: This pattern standardizes interactions between an agent and external tools, ensuring that the agent can effectively use these tools and handle any errors.

cards.title: Analogy: Using a Toolbox
cards.body: Think of the Tool Use Pattern like having a toolbox. Just like you need to know which tool to use and how to fix it if it breaks, agents need clear methods for using tools and handling errors.

cards.title: Example of Tool Use Pattern
cards.body: If an agent wants to use a weather tool, it first checks if the tool is available, then makes a request, and if there’s an error, it knows how to handle it.

cards.title: Myth: Tools Can Be Used Any Way
cards.body: Some believe agents can use tools without a clear method. In reality, a structured approach is essential for effective tool use.

cards.title: Comparing Tool Use Patterns
cards.body: Visualizing how agents handle tools can clarify their operational structure.

left.text: Agent uses tool and manages errors.

right.text: Agent uses tool randomly without checks.

exercises.prompt: What is a key aspect of the Tool Use Pattern?

options.text: Using as many tools as possible

exercises.explain: The Tool Use Pattern is about standardizing how agents interact with tools to ensure effective use and error management.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Agents should handle errors from tools in any random way.
exercises.explain: Agents must have a structured way to handle errors, ensuring they can manage any issues that arise.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: The Tool Use Pattern helps agents declare, call, and handle ______ from external tools.
exercises.sentence: The Tool Use Pattern helps agents declare, call, and handle errors from external tools.
exercises.explain: In this context, 'errors' represents the mishaps agents must manage when interacting with tools.

exercises.prompt: Which statement best describes a feature of the Tool Use Pattern?

options.text: Agents can use tools without checks.
Agents should standardize how they communicate with tools.
Agents do not need to manage tool errors.

exercises.explain: Standardizing how agents communicate with tools ensures they can effectively utilize these tools and deal with any errors.

exercises.prompt: What do agents gain by standardizing their interaction with tools according to the Tool Use Pattern?

options.text: They can use tools more effectively and manage errors.
They can randomly select tools to use.
They will only use one tool at a time.

exercises.explain: Standardizing interactions allows agents to use tools more effectively and manage any errors, leading to better outcomes in their tasks.
